title: Anomaly on Outbound Bytes from Database Subnet

spl:
index=network sourcetype=zeek:conn id.orig_h=10.50.* earliest=-7d
| timechart span=1h sum(orig_bytes) as b
| anomalydetection b action=annotate
description: Uses statistical anomaly detection on east-west bytes for adverse event triage.
value: Uses statistical anomaly detection on east-west bytes for adverse event triage.
implementation: (1) Label DB VLANs; (2) suppress backup windows; (3) integrate with IR runbook.
cimSpl: | tstats summariesonly=t sum(All_Traffic.bytes_in) as agg_value from datamodel=Network_Traffic.All_Traffic by All_Traffic.action, All_Traffic.src, All_Traffic.dest, All_Traffic.dest_port span=1h | sort - agg_value
knownFalsePositives: Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
grandmaExplanation: Uses statistical anomaly detection on east-west bytes for adverse event triage — so you can show exactly what is being watched and when something went wrong.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Zeek `conn`.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `zeek:conn`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Label DB VLANs; (2) suppress backup windows; (3) integrate with IR runbook.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=network sourcetype=zeek:conn id.orig_h=10.50.* earliest=-7d
| timechart span=1h sum(orig_bytes) as b
| anomalydetection b action=annotate
```

Understanding this SPL

**Anomaly on Outbound Bytes from Database Subnet (DE.AE-02)** — Uses statistical anomaly detection on east-west bytes for adverse event triage.

Documented **Data sources**: `zeek:conn`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Zeek `conn`. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: network; **sourcetype**: zeek:conn. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=network, sourcetype=zeek:conn, time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `timechart` plots the metric over time using **span=1h** buckets — ideal for trending and alerting on this use case.
• Pipeline stage (see **Anomaly on Outbound Bytes from Database Subnet (DE.AE-02)**): anomalydetection b action=annotate

Optional CIM / accelerated variant (same use case, normalized fields via Common Information Model):

```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t sum(All_Traffic.bytes_in) as agg_value from datamodel=Network_Traffic.All_Traffic by All_Traffic.action, All_Traffic.src, All_Traffic.dest, All_Traffic.dest_port span=1h | sort - agg_value
```

Understanding this CIM / accelerated SPL

**Anomaly on Outbound Bytes from Database Subnet (DE.AE-02)** — Uses statistical anomaly detection on east-west bytes for adverse event triage.

Documented **Data sources**: `zeek:conn`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Zeek `conn`. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

This **CIM or accelerated** block uses normalized field names and/or `tstats` over data models. Enable **acceleration** on the referenced models (and correct CIM knowledge objects) or the search may return nothing.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Uses `tstats` against accelerated summaries for data model `Network_Traffic.All_Traffic` — enable acceleration for that model.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Enable Data Model Acceleration (and metric indexes for `mstats`) for the models or datasets referenced above; otherwise `tstats`/`mstats` may return no results from summaries.


Step 3 — Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Time chart, Anomaly overlay
